reasoning: The evidence strongly suggests Greenland will NOT announce an independence referendum date before July 1, 2025. Analysis: 1) The winning party (Demokraatit/Democrats) takes a 'gradual approach' to independence, focusing first on making self-government work (search_0_result_0, search_1_result_0). 2) Demokraatit leader Jens-Frederik Nielsen formed a broad coalition that EXCLUDES Naleraq - the party pushing for a quick independence vote - specifically because Democrats were 'unwilling to begin formal negotiations' on independence (search_2_summary). 3) Expert analysis puts referendum timeline at 2-10 years 'down the line' (search_1_summary). 4) Nielsen's coalition controls 23 of 31 seats, so doesn't need to compromise on independence timeline. 5) The phrase 'not any time soon' from multiple analysts (search_0_result_7) reflects the consensus view. 6) With only ~2 months between knowledge cutoff (May 2) and July 1 deadline, and the government having just formed in late March, the timeline is extremely tight for such a major announcement. The 8% accounts for remote tail risk of unforeseen developments, but the political configuration makes an announcement highly improbable.
